differential_diagnoses: Systemic onset juvenile idiopathic arthritis (adult-onset Still's disease)
Occult malignancy (e.g., lymphoma or metastatic disease)
next_steps: Obtain thyroid function tests (TSH, free T4, T3) and inflammatory markers (CRP, ESR).
Perform a targeted thyroid and neck ultrasound to evaluate for thyroiditis vs. abscess or mass.
Perform a radioactive iodine uptake scan to distinguish thyroiditis from hyperthyroid conditions.
Consider fine-needle aspiration (FNA) biopsy if a suspicious nodule is identified on ultrasound to rule out malignancy or abscess.
Initiate nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs) for symptom management of suspected subacute thyroiditis.
Perform blood cultures for atypical organisms if fever persists despite treatment.
